::: top-banner
:::

# Workshop overview

This workshop brings together marine megafauna taxonomic experts, climate and ocean modelers, and regional implementation partners participating in Save the Blue Five.

The meeting will build on the technical foundations established in previous workshops and create a shared space to discuss progress, data inputs, uncertainty, information gaps, and next steps for regional collaboration.

The main goal is to review and refine ongoing analyses, including species distribution models, climate vulnerability assessment, and related products that can support science and decision-making in the Southeast Pacific.

## Location and dates

- San Cristobal Island, Galápagos
- June 8 to June 12, 2026

## About this site

This website was created with Quarto and is structured as a working hub for workshop logistics, agenda, participants, and resources.
